rows.notes: Page title clearly centered at top. Handwriting legible enough to read as 'Arrearages Received Novr die 2do 1767'. Minor uncertainty on exact Latin/abbrev forms but meaning is clear.
Clear entry text 'Weston' and amount columns well aligned. Pounds = 4, shillings = 11, pence = 4 are crisply written and within valid ranges.
Indented line with same abbreviation; numeric read as 4 pounds, 2 shillings, 4 pence. Vertical alignment clear. Minor ambiguity in reading 'Mech' but numbers are clear.
Description 'Chace Hill' legible. Pounds 17, shillings 2, pence 9 clearly aligned; no fraction. All values validate.
Place name partly ambiguous (read as 'Yardington'). Amount reads 5 pounds exactly (5 0 0). Description handwriting slightly smudged but context (place + recd) supports reading.

Description 'Tenem' (possibly 'Tennen' or similar) legible enough. Amount appears as 8 pounds, 14 shillings, 5½ pence (pence shows a small trailing loop interpreted as halfpenny). Slight smudge on fraction but alignment consistent.
Place name uncertain ('Wilnot/Wilmot'); amount read as 5 pounds, 0 shillings, 10 pence. Pence digit slightly narrow and could be 10 or 3, but alignment with column favors 10.

Indented 'Capon pro ditto'. Pounds 13 and shillings 19 clear. Pence looks like '3 3/4' (three and three‑quarters of a penny / 3q) — fractional mark is faint; read as 3 + 3q (0.75). Slight uncertainty on fraction shape.
Description 'Kidlington Mills' legible. Amount 6-19-8 clear and within valid ranges. Ink slightly heavier on shillings but unambiguous.
Similar pattern to previous line; 'Garrington Lodge' reading somewhat unclear on first letter but amount 6-19-8 is clear and aligned with column lines.

Sub-item 'Mohneux ditto'. Pounds and shillings readable as 10 and 9. Pence appears '11 1/4' (three figures with a small trailing 'q' style loop) — fraction reading uncertain (could be 1/4 or 1/2); chosen 0.25 because loop resembles 'q' (farthing) more than halfpenny.
Place name reading uncertain ('Tintinhull Nesper' approximate). Amount 1-15-0 is clearly formed in columns. Description letters faint but sufficient to identify as header/entry.
Sub-item 'Holwell' read; pounds 26 and shillings 14 clear. Pence reads '6 1/2' with a distinct halfpenny loop. Slightly lower confidence due to heavier ink on adjoining lines.
Name 'Clifton Gorry' readable. Amount 2-1-4 is small but clear and within valid ranges.
Description partly abbreviated ('South New. Tenem'). Amount looks like 5-9-7½ (pence shows halfpenny mark). The halfpenny is somewhat faint, hence reduced confidence.
Place name 'Bennington' legible. Amount 3-6-8 clear in columns. No fraction visible.
Place-name uncertain (read as 'Lodlacre Conway' or similar). Numeric 6-13-4 is legible. Slight ambiguity in letters of description but amounts align well.
Sub-item 'Harforth' read. Amount 1-13-4 clear. Ink consistent with neighboring amounts.
Name 'Greenhow' plausible. Amount 5-14-3 clearly aligned. Minor uncertainty in first letter of name but numbers are unambiguous.
Description looks like 'Taylor major' or 'Taylor, major Lot' — uncertain. Amount reads 4-9-3½ (pence shows halfpenny loop). Slight ambiguity in name reduces confidence.

Name appears 'Fardon olim Calcott' (olim = formerly). Pounds column blank/zero, shillings read as 15, pence as 9¼ (farthing). The fractional farthing mark is faint and could be 1/2, but alignment and small loop suggest q (¼).
First name unclear ('Munichin' or 'Munchen'); reads 'olim Barnett'. Amount 3-6-8 visible but somewhat faint; chosen reading based on column alignment.
Description reads 'Little Gen Quit Rents' (abbreviated). Pounds and shillings blank/zero; pence reads as 6 (small number). Low confidence because zeros/pence are small and faint.
Looks like a large entry '85 0 1' (pounds 85, shillings 0, pence 1). The '85' is prominent and clear; pence 1 is small but aligned. Description reading 'Cumnock' has some uncertainty.
Place 'Longwitton' (or similar) and amount 30-0-0 clearly written and unambiguous in the columns.

rows.description: Southnewington Recd pro Direc an: ad Mech:
rows.notes: Large amount reads '255 0 0' clearly. Place/description reading 'Southnewington' and abbreviations are somewhat cramped and faint; numbers are aligned and clear.
Description is highly abbreviated/uncertain ('Gremier', 'Grendier' etc.). Amount 17-10-0 visible. Because the description is ambiguous, confidence lowered though numeric reading is clear.
Short entry, likely 'Sharp annuity'. Amount 2-0-0 is clear. Description abbreviations uncertain; numbers are aligned and unambiguous.
Entry reads 'Barnston pro Bien'. Amount 1-0-0 clearly written. Description letters legible enough to identify place.
Final visible entry 'Mountcastle' (or 'Mountacute') — reading slightly uncertain but numeric 1-0-0 clear and aligned.
